results.title: Progress: the story of the Rootes Group
results.creator_credit: Cecil Musk and Edward W. Beckett
results.brief_description: Traces the history of the Rootes Group of factories, manufacturers of automobiles and aircraft. Shows the Rootes workshop 40 years ago; the use of mechanized equipment in WW1; amalgamation with the Humber organization; rationalization of the Rootes group resulting in decreased production costs and increased quality of products. The major part of the film illustrates the magnitude of the part played by the Rootes group in manufacturing armoured cars, trucks, bombers, and fighters during WW2.

results.title: Progress with precision
results.brief_description: Visits various industrial centres in England and shows how Capstan and Turret Lathes are made. All the elaborate components are examined, along with the finished machines.

results.title: Progress reported: the story of the Irish peat industry
results.creator_credit: Joe Mendoza, Vincent Corcoran and Irish Tourist Board
results.brief_description: An account of the growth of the Irish peat industry. Peat is now gathered by machinery and the industry provides employment for many people. The many uses of peat are explained - for heating, industry, and electricity.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Joe Mendoza, Vincent Corcoran and Irish Tourist Board

results.title: Programmed learning TEMAC in use
results.brief_description: Explains the theory behind the experimental adoption of programmed learning in a high school. Shows how programmed learning using TEMAC offers a creative, new approach to both learning, unlike the old lock-step method with the whole class proceeding together. After analysis of the act of learning, the material, ninth grade Algebra, is presented, psychologically arranged into segments, shows in one frame at a time, so that the first step must be taken individually before it is possible to take the second. Shows 34 8 th grade pupils, working through the course, and also how the method can be applied in language laboratories and in controlling electronic equipment.

results.title: Profit, you gotta be out of your cotton pickin' mind
results.creator_credit: Sam Weiss, Nick Bosustow and Stephen Bosustow Productions
results.brief_description: Making a profit, the essence of business survival, is humorously traced in retelling the story of Eli Whitney and his cotton gin. This animated story illustrates a simple explanation of the need for profit in business.

results.title: Profile: Canada
results.creator_credit: Jim Turpie, Crawley Films and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: This film offers a magnificent panorama of Canada from coast to coast. The land, the people, the industries, the pleasures - in fact the entire life of the nation is beautifully filmed in this colorful composite survey.

results.title: Profile of a quartet
results.creator_credit: Humphrey Burton, Peter Newington and BBC-TV
results.brief_description: A glimpse of the work which goes into a production of good chamber music. Introduces the members of the Quartet both at home with their families and at rehearsal together where much discussion takes place and several pieces of chamber music are played. Illustrates the focussing of the four men’s lives to produce this close teamwork.
